How to plan and track your tasks using GitHub

June 2, 2022

Who knew that project management can be done on GitHub too! Open source brings endless possibilities, and I started grasping it. Thank you, Ravin, for showing me and helping to organise the project in this way.

  1. Create a repository for gsoc2022 in your GitHub repository.
  2. Go to the Projects tab and press Add project.

  3. Go to your profile to create a new project and choose Create project. It will lead you to the corresponding page.

  4. Rename your project and add your mentors and team members in Settings -> Manage access.
  5. You should go back to the Projects tab and once you press Add project your Project should appear. It takes a bit longer for the Project to be generated and to appear in the dropdown list.
  6. Now, go to the Issues tab and add a new issue that will be linked to the Project.

  7. As you can see below, you can name the issue, add more details, assign it to yourself or your mentors, label it (there are several predefined categories), and add it to your newly created Project!

  8. If you check your Project’s page, you will see your added task. Think of this as the main dashboard that shows the progress of your to-do lists.

Good luck in organising your tasks 😊

To read more about the beta version, follow this link.
